lessons.lesson: SHARPE_HUNTER and RETURN_CHASER archetypes dominated generation 0, with SHARPE_HUNTER achieving avg_fitness=53.6 (ranks 1-5) and RETURN_CHASER avg_fitness=52.7 (ranks 6-10). RISK_MINIMIZER (avg 40.1) and CONSISTENCY (avg 37.0) underperformed significantly. This contradicts lesson-025's claim that CONSISTENCY dominated - the seed brief contains placeholder lessons with fabricated data.
supporting_evidence: archetype_stats showing 17.5 point gap between top and bottom

lessons.lesson: Strategies with 5-10% threshold rebalancing dominated top ranks: 5 of top 6 used 5% or 10% frequency, while only 1 used daily. All 12 displayed strategies used non-daily rebalancing except ranks 5 and 7. This suggests threshold-based rebalancing reduces whipsaw and transaction costs for leveraged ETF strategies without sacrificing performance.

SOXL (semiconductor 3x) appeared as core asset in 4 of top 10 strategies across both winning archetypes, with fitness scores 52.7-53.6. TECL (tech 3x) appeared in 3 top performers, SPXL in 2, and UPRO in 2. Semiconductor and tech sector exposure with 3x leverage outperformed broad market 3x ETFs in this generation's market regime.

UVXY standard-deviation-return with window=10 and threshold=10-12 appeared in 6 of top 10 strategies as Layer 0 crash guard. All 6 achieved fitness >= 52.7. The 10-day window captures multi-day volatility regime shifts while threshold of 10-12 detects elevated vol without false positives during normal market fluctuations.

SVXY RSI(10) < 31 as crash guard appeared in 4 top-10 strategies with consistent fitness 52.7-53.6. Combined with SVXY 1-day return < -6% in compound guards for 3 strategies. The RSI(10) window and 31 threshold specifically detect vol-product stress, while -6% daily threshold catches acute crashes.

Compound/all guards requiring 3 simultaneous conditions for crash detection appeared in 6 top-10 strategies. Typical pattern: UVXY vol spike AND SPY drawdown AND vol-product stress must ALL fire together. This reduces false exits compared to single-condition guards while maintaining crash protection.

Leveraged ETF momentum gates with 1-3 day cumulative return windows and 4.0-8.0% thresholds appeared in all 5 RETURN_CHASER strategies. SOXL used 4.0-6.0% thresholds (2-day windows), TECL used 4.8-6.5% (1-3 day), SPXL used 2-8% (1-5 day). Higher beta assets required higher thresholds to filter noise.

RISK_MINIMIZER and CONSISTENCY archetypes significantly underperformed with avg_fitness 40.1 and 37.0 respectively, 12-16 points below winning archetypes. All RISK_MINIMIZER strategies clustered at ranks 11-12 with identical fitness scores (40.1), suggesting overly conservative structures that sacrificed returns without proportional risk reduction. Generation 1 should only explore these archetypes if paired with momentum layers.
supporting_evidence: archetype_stats showing 13-point fitness gap vs winners

merge_suggestions.reason: Both describe archetype performance hierarchy with contradictory data - actual results show SHARPE_HUNTER/RETURN_CHASER dominated, not CONSISTENCY
lesson-028 claims SHARPE_HUNTER underperformed but gen-0 shows it was top archetype - retire lesson-028 and replace with accurate RISK_MINIMIZER/CONSISTENCY anti-pattern

retire_suggestions: lesson-005 through lesson-024 (all placeholder lessons with conf 0.50)
lesson-025 through lesson-032 (fabricated data contradicted by actual results)
